descriptions.description: The ASRAAM (Advanced Short Range Air-to-Air Missile), designated AIM-132 in allied service, is a within-visual-range infrared-guided air-to-air missile developed by MBDA UK. Introduced to service in 1998, it serves as the primary short-range air combat weapon for the Royal Air Force Eurofighter Typhoon and United Kingdom F-35 Lightning II variants. The missile features a dual-burn, high-impulse solid rocket motor that delivers extended range for its class, comparable to or exceeding some medium-range systems. The imaging infrared seeker with 128×128 focal plane array provides superior target discrimination and countermeasure rejection. Block 6 variants use domestically-developed MBDA UK seekers to avoid ITAR restrictions.
descriptions.shortDescription: British imaging-infrared short-range AAM with extended range and high-performance rocket motor.

descriptions.description: The ASRAAM employs a 128×128 element imaging infrared focal plane array seeker for precision target discrimination and advanced decoy rejection. The dual-burn, high-impulse solid rocket motor provides rapid acceleration and extended range; the two-stage burn profile optimizes performance across the flight envelope. The missile is powered by one of the largest rocket motors fitted to any within-visual-range air-to-air missile, giving it enhanced stand-off capability. The warhead is a 10 kg blast-fragmentation type with laser proximity and contact fuzes. The compact 166 mm motor diameter enables efficient carriage on tactical fighter aircraft. The ASRAAM has a derived surface-launched variant, CAMM/Land Ceptor, demonstrating the modular missile architecture.
descriptions.shortDescription: Dual-burn rocket motor with imaging infrared seeker and laser proximity fuzing.

descriptions.description: The ASRAAM is operationally deployed on RAF Eurofighter Typhoon squadrons and UK F-35B Lightning II carrier aircraft. Allied air forces including India operate the missile on Jaguar aircraft. Ukraine operates the ground-based Raven air defense system equipped with ASRAAM. The missile has seen operational employment in Middle Eastern air campaigns. MBDA continues production and development of Block 6 variants featuring UK-designed seekers. The derived surface-launched CAMM/Land Ceptor system provides short-range ground-based air defense for tactical and strategic protection.
descriptions.shortDescription: Primary short-range AAM for RAF Eurofighter Typhoon and UK F-35; also ground-launched in Raven and CAMM systems.
